candidates.rationale: The bowl is currently in the gripper and is the wrong container; setting it down frees the gripper to retrieve the correct water-filled pot.
The earlier failure came from using the bowl instead of the pot, so grabbing the pot is the most direct corrective step toward watering the plant.
If the bowl somehow contains water despite the failed attempt, pouring it into the plant could still achieve the goal immediately.
Placing the bowl into the sink clears it from the gripper/workspace and positions it away from the plant while preparing to use the pot.
Picking up another container is a fallback if the pot is not readily accessible, allowing the robot to fetch water for the plant by another route.
